name: chia-base

metadata.name: chia-base
metadata.summary: Common types and simple utilities used through chia code base

documentation: chia_base: base data types for chia coins, spends and contract development

modules.name: chia_base.meta.typing
documentation: `GenericAlias` was introduced in python 3.9

functions.name: chia_base.meta.optional.optional_from_union
documentation:
Python typing considers `X | None` and `None | X` to be the same types.
We normalize unions here and fetch the `X` type, or return `None` if the
union doesn't represent an `Optional`.

classes.name: chia_base.meta.type_tree.TypeTree

methods.name: chia_base.meta.type_tree.TypeTree.__call__
documentation:
Recursively descend a "type tree" invoking the appropriate functions.

This function is helpful for run-time building a complex function that operates
on a complex type out of simpler functions that operate on base types.

`simple_type_lookup`: a type to callable look-up. Must return a `T` value.
`compound_type_lookup`: recursively handle compound types like `list` and `tuple`.
`other_f`: a function to take a type and return a `T` value

modules.name: chia_base.util.bech32
documentation: the API to `contrib.bech32m` is an abomination unto man. This API is slightly less bad

functions.name: chia_base.util.bech32.bech32_decode
documentation:
Return `None` if no valid bech32 could be extracted, or the
prefix, data, encoding as a tuple.

functions.name: chia_base.util.bech32.bech32_encode
documentation: Convert the given blob to bech32 or bech32m (as per `encoding`).

functions.name: chia_base.util.std_hash.std_hash
documentation: The standard sha256 hash used in many places.

modules.name: chia_base.cbincode
documentation:
Implement `cbincode` streaming and parsing.

This is a very simple serialization standard. It's almost identical to the
bincode standard implemented by a rust crate and described here:

https://github.com/bincode-org/bincode/blob/trunk/docs/spec.md

It uses `FixintEncoding`, ie. a fixed size for each of `(uint/int)(8|16|32)`.
The bincode standard uses uint32 for size prefixes (on lists, for example) and
the cbincode standard uses uint16. Other than that, the encoding has very few
surprises.


Parsers and streamers are created at runtime based on a type.

Supported types include:

- `bytes`, `str`
- any class with a `.parse` class function
  - this includes, `(u)?int(8|16|32)`, `bytes32`
- `list[T]` where `T` is supported
- `tuple[T1, T2, ..., TN]` where each `Tn` is supported
- `Optional[T]` where `T` is supported (also spelled `T | None`)
- classes decorated with `@dataclass` where each field is of a supported type

Transitive closures of the above list are also supported.

classes.name: chia_base.core.coin.Coin
documentation: This structure is used in the body for the reward and fees genesis coins.

documentation: Internal function that computes the Bech32 checksum.

functions.name: chia_base.contrib.bech32m.bech32_hrp_expand
documentation: Expand the HRP into values for checksum computation.

functions.name: chia_base.contrib.bech32m.encode

classes.name: chia_base.contrib.bech32m.Encoding
documentation: Enumeration type to list the various supported encodings.

classes.name: chia_base.core.coin_spend.CoinSpend
documentation: This represents a coin spend on the chia blockchain.

modules.name: chia_base.core.conlang
documentation: signature conditions

modules.name: chia_base.cbincode.streamer
documentation:
Create a streamer function at runtime based on the type passed in. Supported types:
- `bytes`, `str`
- any class with a `._class_stream` class function or `.stream` member function
  - this includes, `(u)?int(8|16|32)`, `bytes32`
- `list[T]` where `T` is supported
- `tuple[T1, T2, ..., TN]` where each `Tn` is supported
- `Optional[T]` where `T` is supported (also spelled `T | None`)
- classes decorated with `@dataclass` where each field is of a supported type
  (these are essentially converted to a `tuple`)

functions.name: chia_base.cbincode.streamer.streamer_for_dataclass
documentation:
Generate a streamer function by iterating over all members of a class.
First, we convert the object to a tuple. Then we serialize the tuple using
the existing tuple serializer already written.

functions.name: chia_base.cbincode.streamer.extra_streamers
documentation:
deal with `dataclass` objects and objects that have a `.stream` object method
or a `._class_stream` class method

functions.name: chia_base.cbincode.streamer.streamer_type_tree
documentation:
Return a `TypeTree[StreamFunction]` that's able to create `cbincode` streamer
for many different types.

classes.name: chia_base.core.spend_bundle.SpendBundle
documentation:
This is a list of coins being spent along with their solution programs, and a single
aggregated signature. This is the object that most closely corresponds to a bitcoin
transaction (although because of non-interactive signature aggregation, the
boundaries between transactions are more flexible than in bitcoin).

modules.name: chia_base.bls12_381
documentation:
The `chia_rs` wheel has some rough edges in its api. This module smooths them over
with a more ergonomic api.

modules.name: chia_base.cbincode.parser
documentation:
Create a parser function at runtime based on the type passed in. Supported types:
- `bytes`, `str`
- any class with a `.parse` class function
  - this includes, `(u)?int(8|16|32)`, `bytes32`
- `list[T]` where `T` is supported
- `tuple[T1, T2, ..., TN]` where each `Tn` is supported
- `Optional[T]` where `T` is supported (also spelled `T | None`)
- classes decorated with `@dataclass` where each field is of a supported type
  (these are essentially converted to a `tuple`)

functions.name: chia_base.cbincode.parser.parser_type_tree
documentation:
Return a `TypeTree[ParseFunction]` that's able to create `cbincode` parser
for many different types.

modules.name: chia_base.bls12_381.secret_key_utils
documentation:
Some secret key utilities that need to know the group order and return `chia_rs`
structures.

classes.name: chia_base.bls12_381.bls_signature.BLSSignature
documentation:
This wraps the chia_rs version and resolves a couple edge cases
around aggregation and validation.

modules.name: chia_base.atoms
documentation:
This module defines some useful atomic types for building more complex
data structures.

Except for `hexbytes` (which is just a hex-printing subclass of `bytes`,
these types are all fixed-sized, simplifying serializing and parsing, like
those used in `cbincode` (the slight modification to the `bincode` standard that
chia uses).

classes.name: chia_base.atoms.hexbytes.hexbytes
documentation:
This is a subclass of bytes that prints itself out as hex,
which is much easier on the eyes for binary data that is very non-ascii .

modules.name: chia_base.atoms.ints
documentation:
Some ints of fixed size. Their fixed size makes them easier to parse and serialize.

Each implements these two class methods:

*  `.parse(f: BinaryIO)`
*  `._class_stream(obj, f: BinaryIO)`

classes.name: chia_base.bls12_381.bls_secret_exponent.BLSSecretExponent

methods.name: chia_base.bls12_381.bls_secret_exponent.BLSSecretExponent.from_seed
documentation:
convert from a seed using the specification at
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-irtf-cfrg-bls-signature-05

This is essentially an `int` with convenience functions.

We don't subclass `int` because we have a different implementation of
`__bytes__` which could cause confusion.

classes.name: chia_base.bls12_381.bls_public_key.BLSPublicKey
documentation:
This corresponds to an element in bls12-381's G1 group, represented
when serialized by a 48-byte x element (with a few extra bits at the
beginning for metadata).

classes.name: chia_base.atoms.sized_bytes.SizedBytes
documentation:
Intended to be subclassed, this base class makes it easy to create
subclasses of `bytes` that require the length to be a specific value.

Having a specific number of bytes means we can easily parse and stream

classes.name: chia_base.atoms.sized_bytes.bytes32
documentation: A subclass of `bytes` that requires the length to be 32.

classes.name: chia_base.atoms.struct_stream.struct_stream
documentation:
This is a base class. Subclasses should define `cls.PACK` as a struct.pack
template string. In return, you get implementations of `parse` and
`_class_stream`.
